title: Which countries include mumps vaccines in their national vaccination programs?
subtitle: This shows which countries provide and recommend mumps vaccines through routine services. People may still be able to receive the vaccine if it's not in the routine schedule – it might be optional or available commercially.
caption:
Which countries include mumps vaccines in their national vaccination programs?

This shows which countries provide and recommend mumps vaccines through routine services. People may still be able to receive the vaccine if it's not in the routine schedule – it might be optional or available commercially.

The world map displays the status of mumps vaccine inclusion in national vaccination programs for the year 2024, categorized by four distinct visual bands. The teal color, representing countries where the vaccine is administered across the "Entire country," covers the majority of the landmass shown, including North America, South America, Europe, Russia, China, Australia, and Japan. In contrast, the salmon-pink band, indicating regions where the vaccine is "Not routinely administered," dominates the African continent, the Middle East, and South Asia, encompassing nations such as India, Pakistan, and Nigeria. A small number of territories, including Greenland, are marked with diagonal hatching to signify "No data." The legend includes a category for "Regions of the country," colored in orange, though no nations on this specific map are shaded with that designation. The visualization presents a clear geographic divide, with routine administration concentrated in the Americas, Europe, and East Asia, while large contiguous areas in Africa and Asia fall into the non-routine category. The data reflects the global landscape of routine immunization schedules as of 2024, noting that improved mumps vaccines first became available in 1967.
